keywords.why: Canva is the #1 beginner design tool for new Etsy printable sellers. Searchers are stuck in Canva and don't know what canvas dimensions map to Etsy frame sizes, what DPI Canva actually exports at, or why their prints come out pixelated. No dedicated page in the registry covers this tool-bridge — snaptosize-vs-canva is a comparison page, not a workflow guide. Clear conversion angle: Canva's fixed single-canvas workflow cannot generate all ratio-packs Etsy sellers need; SnapToSize finishes the job Canva can't.

faqs.q: What size canvas should I use in Canva for Etsy printables?
faqs.a: Start with a 6000×4500 pixel custom canvas in Canva — this is a 4:5 master at 300 DPI that scales down cleanly to 8×10, 16×20, 11×14, and other 4:5 sizes. For 2:3 Etsy prints, use 6000×4000 px. For A-series (ISO), use 6250×8839 px (A3 at 300 DPI). Always export as PDF Print for true 300 DPI.

No. Canva's PNG and JPG exports default to 96 DPI — fine for screens but pixelated when printed. You have to export as PDF Print to get true 300 DPI output. Canva Pro lets you toggle PNG at higher resolutions, but PDF Print is the safest 300 DPI path for Etsy printables.

faqs.q: Can you make all Etsy print sizes in Canva?
faqs.a: Not efficiently. Canva uses a single fixed canvas per design — you'd need to duplicate and resize the design for every ratio (2:3, 3:4, 4:5, ISO), which multiplies your work. Most sellers design once in Canva at a 4:5 master, then use a sizing tool like SnapToSize to auto-generate the rest.

faqs.q: What DPI does Canva export PNG at?
faqs.a: Canva Free exports PNG at 96 DPI (screen resolution). Canva Pro allows PNG export at custom DPI up to 300. For Etsy printables, either upgrade to Pro or use PDF Print export — which is 300 DPI on all plans.

faqs.q: Can I sell Canva designs on Etsy?
faqs.a: Yes, if the design is your own creation. Canva's content license allows selling finished designs, but you cannot resell Canva's stock templates or elements as-is. Your own illustrations, quotes, or layouts made in Canva are fine to sell as printables.

faqs.q: Why do my Canva prints look pixelated when buyers print them?
faqs.a: Two common causes: (1) you exported as PNG at 96 DPI instead of PDF Print at 300 DPI; (2) your Canva canvas was too small — if you designed at 2000×2500 px and a buyer prints at 16×20, that's only 125 DPI, which looks fuzzy. Always design at minimum 4800×6000 px for 16×20 prints.

faqs.q: Can Canva create all 5 ratio packs automatically for Etsy?
faqs.a: No. Canva has no batch-export-by-ratio feature. You'd need to manually resize the canvas for every ratio (2:3, 3:4, 4:5, ISO) and re-export each one. SnapToSize takes a single Canva export and generates all five ratio packs in one upload.

faqs.q: What's the Canva PDF Print export size limit for Etsy?
faqs.a: Canva PDF Print exports have no hard size limit, but Etsy listings cap each file at 20MB. A Canva PDF at 300 DPI for a 24×36 print can easily exceed 20MB — export to JPEG at 90% quality, or use a sizing tool that auto-compresses to stay under the Etsy limit.

Kitchen fills the obvious gap in the niche-vertical cluster and matches the proven bathroom/kids template pattern.

faqs.q: What sizes sell best for kitchen wall art on Etsy?
faqs.a: 8×10, 11×14, and 5×7 are the top-selling kitchen sizes in the US. For galley kitchens and coffee bars, 8×10 portrait dominates. For above-the-table placement in open-plan kitchens, 16×20 and 11×14 landscape sell well. A4 is essential for international kitchens.

faqs.q: Should kitchen wall art be portrait or landscape?
faqs.a: Both — kitchen placement varies more than other rooms. Portrait prints suit narrow walls beside the fridge, coffee bars, and galley kitchens. Landscape prints work above dining tables, kitchen islands, and stove backsplashes. Offering both ratios in every listing prevents lost sales.

faqs.q: What size print works above a kitchen table?
faqs.a: 16×20 and 11×14 landscape are the most common sizes above kitchen tables — large enough to anchor the space without overwhelming it. For a three-piece set above a wider table, 8×10 landscape prints work best. Avoid smaller than 8×10 for above-table placement; they look lost.

faqs.q: What pixel dimensions do I need for an 11×14 kitchen print at 300 DPI?
faqs.a: 11×14 at 300 DPI requires a file that is 3,300 × 4,200 pixels. This is a common coffee-bar and above-shelf size — design at least this resolution so it stays crisp when a buyer prints it.

faqs.q: Do kitchen buyers want A4 prints?
faqs.a: Yes — A4 (210×297mm) is the standard print size across Europe, the UK, and Australia. Every major IKEA RIBBA/KNOPPÄNG frame matches A4. Including A4 alongside your US sizes typically adds 20–30% more international orders for kitchen-printable listings.

faqs.q: What sizes should I offer for a 3-piece kitchen art set?
faqs.a: Most 3-piece kitchen sets sell in 8×10 or 11×14 — consistent size across all three pieces so buyers can frame them identically. Offer each piece in multiple ratios (4:5 and 2:3) so the buyer picks what fits their frames. Never mix sizes within a single 3-piece listing.

faqs.q: Can I sell square kitchen wall art on Etsy?
faqs.a: Yes — 12×12 and 8×8 square prints are popular for kitchen coffee bars and shelf displays, especially for minimalist or geometric designs. Offer square alongside standard ratios, not instead of them — most buyers still want portrait 4:5 for framing.
